footer.ctaHeading: Ready to discover Carnac the easy, enjoyable way?
footer.ctaBody: Climb aboard the Petit Train de Carnac for a guided tour blending history, scenery and comfort. Take in the famous Carnac standing stones, the white-sand beaches and the harbour of La Trinité-sur-Mer — no effort required, with clear audio commentary throughout.
footer.tagline: The Petit Train de Carnac offers a guided tour through one of the most remarkable regions in southern Brittany. Comfortably seated on board, visitors discover Carnac, its famous megaliths, its beaches and the nearby harbour in a relaxed and accessible way.

shared.googleRatingDescription: Le Petit Train de Carnac is rated 4.7 on Google with more than 6,000 reviews — making it one of the most popular tourist attractions in Carnac.
shared.googleRatingDescriptionAlt: Le Petit Train de Carnac is rated above 4.7 on Google with more than 6,000 reviews — making it one of the most popular tourist attractions in Carnac.

hero.label: Le Petit Trains de Morbihan · Carnac
hero.description: The Petit Train de Carnac offers a guided sightseeing tour through the town and its surroundings. Explore the world's most beautiful Neolithic site on this 50-minute guided tour. A simple, comfortable way to discover Carnac, its heritage and its iconic landscapes — no long walks required.
hero.tagline: Ideal for families, couples, seniors and visitors of all ages.
hero.googleBadge: Le Petit Train de Carnac is rated 4.7 on Google with more than 6,000 reviews — making it one of the most popular tourist attractions in Carnac.
hero.rightCardHeading: Guided tour with audio commentary in 16 languages
hero.rightCardBody: French, English, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Russian, Chinese, Japanese, Swedish, Danish, Polish, Arabic, Croatian and Slovenian.
hero.rightImageAlt: Le Petit Train de Carnac on a scenic route
hero.noteImportanteBody: Booking is possible but not required. You can book up to 2 hours before your departure. Remember to allow time for travel and on-site parking. After that, head straight to the departure point and buy your tickets at the kiosk or from the driver.
hero.noteImportanteBodyExtra: Not all seats are available for online sale.

home.title: Le Petit Train de Carnac / Menhir Tour
home.description: Megalith tour of Carnac aboard the Petit Train — 55-minute guided ride, multilingual audio commentary, beaches and the harbour of La Trinité-sur-Mer.

prices.description: Prices for the Carnac megalith tour by Petit Train — €8.50 adult, €5 child, free under 3. Group rates and online booking available.

routes.description: Discover the route of the Petit Train de Carnac: the Carnac menhirs, Carnac-Plage and the port of La Trinité-sur-Mer. A 55-minute guided tour with audio commentary in 16 languages.

prices.heroDescription: Find every price and booking detail for the Petit Train de Carnac. This page lists individual and group tickets, child discounts, and how to book your guided tour with confidence.
prices.reviewQuote: "I didn't expect this much for €8.50. Truly a beautiful experience."
prices.faqDescription: Find clear answers to the most common questions about prices, group discounts and how to book the Petit Train de Carnac.

faq.q1: How much is a ticket for the Petit Train de Carnac?
faq.a1: Prices depend on age and group size. For individual visitors, the standard adult ticket is €8.50. Children under 12 enjoy a reduced rate of €5. Group prices apply from 20 people.
faq.a2: Yes. Children under 12 get a reduced rate. Their ticket gives access to the same guided tour and audio commentary, including a version specially adapted for children.
faq.q3: Do children need a ticket to ride the Petit Train?
faq.a3: Yes. Every passenger must hold a ticket to board the Petit Train de Carnac. Reduced rates apply for children under 12.
faq.a4: Yes. Group rates are available from 20 boarding passengers. These reduced rates apply to both adults and children. Booking in advance is recommended.
faq.q5: What's included in the ticket price?
faq.a5: Each ticket includes the full guided tour of about 55 minutes, multilingual audio commentary, a child-adapted version, and a comfortable on-board experience. No extra fees apply.
faq.q6: Can I buy tickets on site?
faq.a6: Yes. Tickets can be purchased at the kiosk in the Ménec car park, subject to availability. In peak season, advance booking is recommended.
faq.a7: Yes. Online booking is available and recommended, especially during peak periods. Online bookings must be made at least two hours before departure.

careers.heroDescription: Our philosophy is simple: warm, reliable and respectful service for every visitor. We're looking for people who are attentive, professional and passionate about sharing Carnac with our guests.

privatisation.overlayHeading: Hire the Petit Train de Carnac for your event
privatisation.overlayDescription: The Petit Train de Carnac can be privately hired for corporate events, seminars, weddings, school groups or VIP visits. Contact us for a tailored quote.

practicalInfo.label: Information
practicalInfo.heading: Practical Information
practicalInfo.description: To help you plan your sightseeing tour in Carnac, here's the essential information to know before booking.

duration.title: Duration
duration.description: The tour lasts about 55 minutes. This pace lets you discover Carnac comfortably while enjoying the commentary and landscapes — without feeling rushed or tired.

departure.title: Departure
departure.description: Mandatory departure point for groups and individual bookings. Located at the Ménec car park in Carnac, directly opposite the Maison des Mégalithes. The stop is clearly signposted and easy to access.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.description: Departures run from April to September, several times a day. The train operates daily in high season. Check current schedules for exact departure times and seasonal adjustments.

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: The Petit Train is accessible to everyone. Ideal for families with young children, elderly visitors and anyone who'd rather skip long walks while still enjoying the best of Carnac.

payment.title: Payment
payment.description: Tickets can be bought directly on board or at the departure point. Cash and card payments accepted. Group and school rates are available — contact us for a quote.

cta.heading: More information
cta.description: Read our complete guide for ticket prices, payment methods and tips to make the most of your journey through the megaliths.

prices.label: Prices
prices.heading: An affordable adventure for the whole family
prices.description: From solo explorers to large families, find the right ticket for your visit. Take advantage of our special rates for children and families.

individual.title: Individual Tickets
individual.note: For individuals: meeting point is at the departure point; on-site ticket office. Free: children under 3 and dogs.

earlyBird.title: Early-morning departures
earlyBird.note: Reduced rate: €1.50 off per ticket, applicable only to the first morning departures (before 10:20 or 10:30 depending on destination). Tickets available at the on-site ticket office.

group.title: Group Booking
group.text: For group rates, contact us directly:
group.note: For groups: reduced rate from 20 boarding passengers. Advance booking is recommended.

beforeYouBook.heading: Before you book
beforeYouBook.description: Before confirming your booking, take a moment to read the practical information about your Petit Train de Carnac visit. These points will help you have a smooth experience on the day and avoid any uncertainty about timing, departure or how the tour runs.
beforeYouBook.imageAlt: Carnac megaliths at the departure point
beforeYouBook.bullet1: Please arrive at least 15 minutes before departure
beforeYouBook.bullet2: Online bookings must be made at least two hours before departure
beforeYouBook.bullet3: Individual visitors start and end the tour at the Ménec departure point
beforeYouBook.bullet5: Audio commentary is available in several languages, including a children's version
beforeYouBook.bullet6: The Petit Train runs in all weather
beforeYouBook.bullet7: Departure is guaranteed with at least 6 paying adults on board

features.label: Tour Overview
features.heading: A simple, enjoyable way to explore Carnac
features.p1: The Petit Train de Carnac invites you to discover the town at a relaxed pace, comfortably seated on board a sightseeing train designed for visitors who want to enjoy Carnac without effort.
features.p2: During the tour, the audio commentary provides clear and accessible information about the places you pass through, helping you understand Carnac's history, monuments and atmosphere. This sightseeing train is ideal for visitors looking for a pleasant introduction to Carnac and its surroundings.
features.imageAlt: Scenic view from the Petit Train de Carnac
features.f1Desc: Discover the highlights of Carnac comfortably seated on board our sightseeing train, designed for visitors who want to explore effortlessly.
features.f2Desc: Audio commentary is available in 16 languages with hygienic headphones provided, included in the ticket price. A children's audio version is also available.
features.f3Desc: Discover 47 commented points of interest: the Ménec, Kermario and Kerlescan menhir alignments, white-sand beaches and the harbour of La Trinité-sur-Mer in a single picturesque journey.
features.f4Title: Suitable for families, seniors and groups
features.f4Desc: Our train welcomes all ages and group sizes — no long walks, no effort, just a comfortable and enjoyable experience for everyone.

groupBookingCta.label: Group Booking — Private Hire
groupBookingCta.heading: Booking for private tours
groupBookingCta.description: If you're travelling in a group or want to privately hire the Petit Train de Carnac for an event, seminar or school visit, get in touch for a tailored quote.

locations.label: Destinations
locations.heading: Discover our other Petit Train tours in Morbihan
locations.description: Extend your visit to Morbihan with our other sightseeing trains. In Vannes and Quiberon, explore new landscapes, unique heritage and unforgettable moments.

vannes.title: Le Petit Train de Vannes
vannes.tagline: Discover the historic heart of Vannes
vannes.description: Climb aboard the Petit Train de Vannes to take in the medieval ramparts, the harbour and the picturesque alleys of the old town.
vannes.cta: Discover the Petit Train de Vannes

quiberon.title: Le Petit Train de Quiberon
quiberon.description: Hop aboard the Petit Train de Quiberon for an unforgettable coastal tour: beaches, cliffs and traditional villages all in one trip.
quiberon.cta: Discover the Petit Train de Quiberon
quiberon.imageAlt: Coastal views of the Quiberon peninsula

reviews.label: Reviews
reviews.heading: What visitors say about the train
reviews.description: Le Petit Train de Carnac is rated above 4.7 on Google, with more than 6,000 reviews — making it one of the most popular tourist attractions in Carnac.

bookingSection.label: Booking
bookingSection.description: Choose your date and time, select the number of tickets and confirm your booking online. The tour lasts 55 minutes with audio commentary available in 16 languages.

gallery.label: Gallery
gallery.heading: Memories of your visit
gallery.description: A glimpse of what awaits you aboard the Petit Train de Carnac. Click to enlarge.

ourLocation.label: Our Location
ourLocation.heading: How to reach the Petit Train de Carnac
ourLocation.description: The Petit Train de Carnac is easy to reach by car, train or bus. Here are the main departure points and tips for your arrival.
ourLocation.arrivalTipDescription: Please arrive at least 15 minutes before departure.
ourLocation.item1Title: Main departure point — Ménec car park
ourLocation.item1Description: Mandatory departure point for groups and individual bookings. Located at the Ménec car park in Carnac, directly opposite the Maison des Mégalithes.
ourLocation.item2Description: Buy tickets on site from the driver, subject to availability.
ourLocation.item3Description: Cours des Quais bus stop, sea side (before the Alain Barrière roundabout). Buy tickets on site from the driver, subject to availability.
ourLocation.item4Description: If you're coming by car, follow signs to Carnac and the Ménec car park. Parking is free.
ourLocation.item5Description: The nearest station is Auray, about 13 km from Carnac. Buses connect Auray to Carnac in high season.

informationsIntro.label: Important Note
informationsIntro.heading: Online Booking
informationsIntro.description: Booking is possible but not required. You can book up to 2 hours before your departure. Remember to allow time for travel and on-site parking. After that, head straight to the departure point and buy your tickets at the kiosk or from the driver.

informationsCta.label: Group Booking — Private Hire
informationsCta.heading: Booking for private tours
informationsCta.description: If you're travelling in a group or want to privately hire the Petit Train de Carnac for an event, seminar or school visit, get in touch for a tailored quote.

faqsHero.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faqsHero.description: Find answers to the most common questions about the Petit Train de Carnac: duration, prices, route, accessibility and bookings.
faqsHero.cardHeading: Guided tour with audio commentary in 16 languages
faqsHero.languages: French, English, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Russian, Chinese, Japanese, Swedish, Danish, Polish, Arabic, Croatian and Slovenian.

faq.defaultDescription: Find clear answers to the most common questions about the Petit Train de Carnac.

default.q1: What is the Petit Train de Carnac?
default.a1: The Petit Train de Carnac is a guided sightseeing train that travels through Carnac and its surroundings: menhirs, beaches and the port of La Trinité-sur-Mer.
default.q2: How long does the Petit Train de Carnac tour last?
default.a2: The guided tour lasts about 55 minutes total, with multilingual audio commentary throughout the route.
default.q3: Where does the Petit Train de Carnac depart from?
default.a3: The main departure and arrival point is the Ménec car park, opposite the Maison des Mégalithes.
default.q4: What will I see during the Petit Train de Carnac tour?
default.a4: During the tour, you'll pass close to the famous menhir alignments, the Saint-Michel tumulus, Carnac-Plage and the port of La Trinité-sur-Mer.
default.q5: Is the Petit Train de Carnac suitable for children and families?
default.a5: Yes, the Petit Train de Carnac is suitable for families with children, elderly visitors and anyone who prefers to skip long walks.
default.q6: What languages is the guided commentary available in?
default.a6: The audio commentary is available in 16 languages, including French, English, German, Spanish, Italian and Dutch.
default.q7: Do I need to book the Petit Train de Carnac in advance?
default.a7: Booking in advance is recommended, especially in high season. Tickets can also be bought on site, subject to availability.

routesTimeline.label: Tour Timeline
routesTimeline.intro: During the guided tour, the Petit Train de Carnac passes through several key locations, offering a complete overview of Carnac and its surroundings.
routesTimeline.introBold: The Petit Train de Carnac serves the following stops during the tour:
routesTimeline.stop1Bullet1: Located opposite the Maison des Mégalithes
routesTimeline.stop1Bullet3: Mandatory departure point for individual visitors
routesTimeline.stop1Note: This is the only guaranteed boarding point for individual tickets.
routesTimeline.stop1ImageAlt: Parking du Ménec, Carnac — Petit Train departure point
routesTimeline.stop2Bullet2: Ideal for visitors staying on the seaside
routesTimeline.stop2Note: Buy your tickets on site at the time of departure (no booking available).
routesTimeline.stop2ImageAlt: Carnac beach — Port en Drô stop
routesTimeline.stop3Bullet1: Located near the « Cours des Quais » bus stop
routesTimeline.stop3Bullet2: Close to the famous marina and sailing area

faqsSection.q1: What is the Petit Train de Carnac?
faqsSection.a1: The Petit Train de Carnac is a guided tourist train that lets visitors discover Carnac and its surroundings comfortably and accessibly. The tour includes the Carnac menhirs, Carnac beach and the harbour of La Trinité-sur-Mer, with audio commentary throughout the route.
faqsSection.q2: How long does the tour last?
faqsSection.a2: The guided tour lasts about 55 minutes. This duration lets visitors see the main sights of Carnac without long walking distances.
faqsSection.q3: Where does the Petit Train de Carnac depart from?
faqsSection.a3: The main departure and arrival point for individual visitors is the Parking du Ménec in Carnac, opposite the Maison des Mégalithes.
faqsSection.q4: Does the Petit Train run every day?
faqsSection.a4: Yes. The Petit Train de Carnac runs every day from April to the end of October, including Sundays and public holidays.
faqsSection.q5: Does the tour run in bad weather?
faqsSection.a5: Yes. The Petit Train runs in all weather. In rain or strong wind the train is glazed on three sides, so you can fully enjoy your tour.
faqsSection.q6: What will I see during the tour?
faqsSection.a6: During the tour, you'll pass by the Carnac menhirs — including the alignments of Ménec, Kermario and Kerlescan. You'll also discover Carnac beach, its white sand and the harbour of La Trinité-sur-Mer.
faqsSection.q7: What languages is the audio commentary available in?
faqsSection.a7: The guided audio commentary is available in 16 languages: French, English, German, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Dutch, Polish, Russian, Swedish, Danish, Czech, Chinese, Japanese and others. The audio is included in the ticket price — no extra cost. A children's version is also available.
faqsSection.q8: Is the tour suitable for children?
faqsSection.a8: Yes. The Petit Train de Carnac is suitable for children and families. The children's audio commentary makes the experience both fun and educational.
faqsSection.q9Pre: Prices vary by age and group size. Adults and children under 12 have different rates. Full prices are available on the
faqsSection.q10: Do I need to book in advance?
faqsSection.a10: Booking in advance is recommended, especially during busy tourist periods. Online bookings must be made at least two hours before departure.
faqsSection.q11: Can I buy tickets on site?
faqsSection.a11: Yes. Tickets can be bought at the ticket office at the Parking du Ménec, subject to availability.
faqsSection.q12: How early should I arrive before departure?
faqsSection.a12: Visitors are asked to arrive at least 15 minutes before departure to validate their ticket and board.
faqsSection.a13: Yes. Group bookings are available for groups of 20 people or more. Booking in advance is recommended.
faqsSection.q14: Can I privatise the Petit Train for an event?
faqsSection.q14Pre: Yes. The Petit Train de Carnac can be privatised for corporate events, associations, school groups and private occasions. A dedicated request form is available on the

informationsScheduleLabels.operatingPeriodBody: The Petit Train de Carnac runs every day from April to the end of October, including Sundays and public holidays.
informationsScheduleLabels.weatherBody: In rain or strong wind, the train is glazed on three sides, so you can fully enjoy your tour.
informationsScheduleLabels.saturdayRule: Saturdays in July & August: Carnac Plage and La Trinité-sur-Mer departures follow the « orange » low-season schedule.
informationsScheduleLabels.onlineBookingNote: Online booking: only available for departure n°1 — Parking du Ménec.
informationsScheduleLabels.closuresBody: Public holidays follow the regular schedule. The Petit Train is closed from 9 to 16 October and from November to March inclusive.
